data.title: Fuel shock creates buying opportunity for Knight-Swift Transport—Citi Research
data.description:
With increased fuel costs driving a risk-off trade in transportation stocks, the recent pullback in Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX) has created a buying opportunity, leading Citi Research to upgrade the stock to Buy from Neutral with a $64 price target that represents 23% upside from Thursday’s closing price.

While there are macro risks to the sector from higher oil prices, Citi Research analyst Ariel Rosa views Knight-Swift (KNX) as a Buy not only for the attractive entry point, but also for signs that truckload spot rates continue to come in above expectations, defying seasonal Q1 weakness. As the largest truckload carrier in the U.S., Knight-Swift (KNX) is uniquely positioned to benefit from “structural tightening in one-way truckload,” Rosa says in his note to clients.

Additionally, fuel surcharges and the record release of oil from strategic reserves should help insulate the trucking industry from the worst of the fuel shocks that have recently rocked energy-dependent sectors.    

“Knight-Swift, being the nation’s largest truckload carrier, arguably has the most cyclical torque to volume inflection as the end of the freight recession gives way to an upcycle. While we remain concerned about downside risk to near-term earnings given rate pressure, we think KNX and its management are in a good position to navigate the current downturn, with trough earnings limiting downside risks,” Rosa writes.

Shares of Knight-Swift Transportation Holdings (KNX) are fractionally higher into Friday’s open. The stock has gained 11% year-over-year versus -1% for Schneider National (SNDR) and -7% for Werner Enterprises (WERN).

data.title: SA analysts break down the market impact of the ongoing U.S.-Iran war
data.description:
As the conflict between the United States and Iran endures further, investors continue assessing the potential implications for global markets amid lingering uncertainty about how the situation may evolve.

With key questions around escalation, energy supply, and economic stability still unresolved, market participants are closely monitoring developments. Weighing in on the current conflict and its implications for the broader financial market are Seeking Alpha’s analysts. See below what they have to say: 

SA analyst James Foord indicated: “The war in Iran is disrupting markets, and indeed, based on recent developments, it seems like it could stretch out for longer. Equities may have a hard time in the next few months, and we could see some bottlenecks appear, but it's still early to call for an AI recession. Ultimately, I think this could be a good change to add exposure as the correction unfolds,” in Forget Oil: Iran War Could Eventually Trigger AI Recession. 

SA analyst Robert & Sam Kovacs said: “Assumptions of a quick resolution are misplaced; structural and leadership challenges in Iran make a near-term ceasefire highly unlikely… Market optimism underestimates the duration and complexity of the crisis; maintaining energy exposure remains prudent as second-order effects are not yet priced in,” on The Iran War Is Far From Over.

SA analyst Eugenio Catone stated: “Iran’s strategy centers on destabilizing global energy markets by blocking the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to induce inflation and economic strain on Western economies. I expect persistent upward pressure on oil prices and inflation, making long-term bonds, speculative stocks, and cryptocurrencies unattractive in this environment,” in Tehran's Economic Trojan Horse: Using High Inflation To Humble The U.S.

SA analyst Financial Serenity outlined: “Historical analysis shows energy inflation from geopolitical events is typically transitory and only marginally impacts overall inflation… At these levels, if the market were to force a mean reversion, it would have a downside buffer of about -7% to -10% from current values on the S&P 500,” in The Conflict In Iran Is Hiding The Truth About S&P 500. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Oxford Lane Capital prices preferred stock offering for $60M
data.description:
• Oxford Lane Capital (OXLC) on Friday priced an underwritten public offering of 2.4M shares of 8.25% Series 2031 Term Preferred Stock at $25 per share, raising about $60M in gross proceeds.
  • The company granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 0.36M additional preferred shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on March 20, 2026.
  • The net proceeds will be used to repay outstanding debt, including existing preferred stock or unsecured notes, and for general working capital purposes.
  • Lucid Capital Markets and Piper Sandler are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering, while Clear Street, InspereX, and William Blair are lead managers, and Wedbush Securities is co-manager.
  • Shares -3.39%
  • Press release.

data.title: From pitch to portfolio: Goldman Sachs' top World Cup stock ideas
data.description:
Goldman Sachs thinks the FIFA World Cup in North America this summer could be a major catalyst for a wide variety of stocks. The first game is on June 11, and the championship game will be played on July 19. The U.S. will host 78 games, and Mexico and Canada will host 13 games each. 

Analysts think that consumer-facing names should see the biggest tailwind, led by travel, leisure, media, and beverages, as fans flock to host cities and tune in and gather in groups worldwide. Airlines, hotels, and booking platforms have already pointed to a benefit with more international travel booked this summer in comparison to last year. Meanwhile, advertising-exposed media groups and broadcasters could also profit from premium pricing on live sports inventory, while digital platforms capture brand spend tied to real-time engagement and betting. Payment networks and card issuers are also on the list of World Cup beneficiaries as elevated cross-border travel, ticketing, and entertainment spending flow through their systems. 

Among U.S. consumer and travel plays, Goldman Sachs pointed to names such as Booking Holdings (BKNG), Expedia (EXPE), and Airbnb (ABNB) due to strong exposure to accommodation and experiential travel in North American and European corridors. In the airline sector, carriers with robust transatlantic and intra-Americas networks, including Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL), and American Airlines (AAL), were highlighted as key beneficiaries of World Cup-driven passenger demand.

Hotel and casino operators such as Marriott International (MAR), Hilton Worldwide (HLT), and MGM Resorts (MGM) were singled out as well due to their historic trend of strong pricing power around major events and constrained room supply in certain host markets. In payments, Visa (V) and Mastercard (MA) are seen as clear winners. 

In beverages, Goldman Sachs singled out brewers and beverage giants with strong U.S. and European footprints, including companies such as Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), Heineken (HEINY), Coca-Cola (KO), and Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP), noting that major football tournaments have historically coincided with higher on-premise volumes.

The Goldman analyst team also noted that Nike (NKE) is well positioned to leverage its scale, innovation pipeline, and wholesale relationships to capture disproportionate attention and market share within global football. Meanwhile, Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) and (ASO) were highlighted as having both started to incorporate World Cup assortment into their stores.

Other apparel companies leaning into sports that could see an incremental sales lift from World Cup tie-ins include Ralph Lauren (RL), PVH (PVH), and Lululemon (LULU).

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive raises dividend by 1.9% to $0.53 a share
data.description:

data.title: Airbus prepares Kratos drones for European combat system flight tests
data.description:
Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) on Friday said it is preparing to test two uncrewed combat aircraft built by Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS) as part of an effort to deliver a new collaborative combat drone capability to the German Air Force by the end of the decade.

The aircraft, known as the Kratos XQ-58A Valkyrie, are being readied for their first flights with a European-developed mission system at Airbus facilities in Manching, near Munich. Airbus said the aircraft could begin flying later this year as part of development of an Uncrewed Collaborative Combat Aircraft concept it hopes will be operational by 2029.

 

 

Airbus’s (EADSF) (EADSY) initiative comes as militaries around the world are accelerating efforts to incorporate unmanned and increasingly autonomous systems into their arsenals. Defense planners view collaborative combat aircraft as a way to expand airpower without putting additional pilots at risk, while also fielding larger numbers of relatively lower-cost platforms that can operate alongside crewed fighters in high-threat environments.

Integrating a European mission system

Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) is equipping the aircraft with its Multiplatform Autonomous Reconfigurable and Secure system, known as MARS. The architecture includes artificial intelligence software designed to replace certain pilot functions and coordinate groups of manned and unmanned aircraft operating together during missions.

Executives say pairing an existing drone platform with a European command-and-control system could shorten development timelines compared with building a fully new aircraft. Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) officials indicated that the approach is intended to deliver a combat-ready capability more quickly while maintaining European control over key software and mission systems.

The company also emphasized cost considerations, suggesting the system is designed to provide relatively low-cost unmanned combat aircraft that could be fielded in larger numbers.

Partnership with Kratos

The effort reflects a collaboration between Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) and Kratos (KTOS), which developed the Valkyrie as a relatively low-cost unmanned combat aircraft capable of operating alongside crewed fighter jets.

Kratos (KTOS) executives described the program as combining a flight-tested drone with Airbus’ mission software to create a system that could operate alone, in groups of unmanned aircraft or alongside piloted fighters. They also pointed to the concept of deploying such drones in larger quantities to expand combat capacity in high-intensity conflicts.

The Valkyrie first flew in the United States in 2019 and has since undergone additional testing.

Eurofighter integration

Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) is also working to enable the Eurofighter Typhoon to act as a command aircraft for the drones. The company and Rafael Advanced Defense Systems are modifying the Litening 5 targeting pod used on Eurofighters to add new connectivity functions.

Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) said the upgrades, along with smaller avionics updates to the fighter jet, are intended to allow the aircraft to control and coordinate unmanned systems during missions.

Aircraft specifications

The Valkyrie has a length of about 9.1 meters and a wingspan of 8.2 meters. Airbus said the aircraft can travel more than 5,000 kilometers and reach altitudes of roughly 45,000 feet, with a maximum takeoff weight of around three tons.

The aircraft can operate autonomously or under the direction of a crewed fighter such as the Eurofighter. Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) said the drones are designed to carry out both kinetic and non-kinetic missions, including tasks considered too dangerous for pilots.

For the German program, Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) and Kratos (KTOS) are initially focusing on a specific operational role aimed at delivering deployable combat capability within the planned timeline.

data.title: Micron in focus as Wedbush ups price target on stronger-than-expected pricing
data.description:
Micron Technologies (MU) was in the spotlight on Friday as Wedbush Securities reiterated its Outperform rating and raised its price target to $500 from $320, citing stronger-than-expected pricing.

Shares rose 1.4% in premarket trading.

“Pricing in CQ1 has seemingly moved well ahead of expectations set previously by Micron,” analyst Matt Bryson wrote in a note to clients, adding that Micron's guidance for its fiscal second-quarter suggested a roughly 30% increase in the average selling price.

In January, contract pricing for dynamic random access memory and NAND seemed to reflect 50% or more gains in the first-quarter of calendar 2026, but it looks as if some contract pricing has seen triple-digit increases, Bryson added.

“And the setup for CQ2 seems more positive than our previous expectations that both NAND and DRAM would likely climb in the 30%–50% range (though Q2's progression in part depends on where CQ1 concludes).”

Additionally, it seems as if there has been no fall-off in memory trends after the Chinese New Year, Bryson added. “Rather if anything we've seen evidence of a continued lift in requirements and even tighter supply dynamics.”

“Net, with both numbers moving higher and MU trading below what we view as typical peak earnings multiples, we see no reason to shift our positive view on the name (or more broadly the memory industry),” Bryson added.

data.title: Glencore raises hope of reviving Rio Tinto deal - Reuters
data.description:
Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) CEO Gary Nagle hopes the recent rise in coal prices will help rekindle Rio Tinto's (RIO) interest in creating the world's ​biggest mining company, Reuters reported Friday, citing three investors who met with leaders of both companies this week.

The companies held talks earlier this ‌year to forge a $240B company that would combine Glencore's (GLCNF) (GLNCY) marketing business and copper assets with Rio's (RIO) operational expertise to serve fast-growing demand for the metal, but discussions ended last month with no deal; under U.K. rules, Rio cannot restart talks with Glencore for six months.

The three investors said Nagle was optimistic about the prospect of another ​opportunity to agree a deal, according to the report.

Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) is said to believe that Rio's (RIO) valuation of the company was tied to the spot price of key commodities like coal on January 7, the day before talks became public, and thinks a more measured view would have been to also take projected prices ​into account.

Since January 7, coal prices and Glencore's (GLCNF) (GLNCY) shares have surged 26%, while Rio's (RIO) shares have gained 9%, with iron ore ​prices dropping slightly.

Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) shares now represent ~35% of a combined market value in a Glencore-Rio (RIO) tie-up, up from 31.5% when the talks became public and closer ‌to the ⁠40% that Glencore reportedly sought as part of the deal rejected by Rio.

data.title: SentinelOne falls after Q4 results, but analysts remain largely positive on growth
data.description:
Shares of SentinelOne (S) fell about 4% premarket on Friday following its fourth-quarter results, but analysts remained largely positive on future growth.

Morgan Stanley kept its Equal-weight rating but lowered the price target on the stock to $17 from $18.

"S reported FQ4 upside, notably on NNARR [Net New Annual Recurring Revenue] and op margins. [The] Company taking a prudent approach to start the year given geopolitical and new CFO overhang, but generally encouraged by the traction the company is seeing in the marketplace, particularly with AI products," said analysts led by Meta Marshall.

The analysts noted that the Equal-weight rating on SentinelOne, despite an attractive valuation, has largely been due to not seeing a positive catalyst for the company (example: CFO change overhang, lack of material profitability/free cash flow, and larger platform competitors).

However, the analysts said that having set more reasonable guidance for the year, pointing to upside on margins (about 600 basis points expansion guided for the year), and hiring a new CFO (Sonalee Parekh, who has a history of scaling efficiently), they think there are ingredients for the company to outperform as the year goes on, especially as new products continue to gain traction.

"We would look towards RSA to get firmer sense of competitive positioning in AI given number of alternatives, as well as how macro environment develops given geopolitical environment, with both remaining key to getting more positive," said Marshall and her team.

The cybersecurity-focused RSA Conference 2026 is slated to take place from March 23-26 in San Francisco.

Wells Fargo maintained its Equal Weight rating but reduced the price target to $14 from $13.

"FQ4 results & FY27 topline guide met expects [expectations] while margin came in above. To us, greater emphasis on growth > margin would've helped us gain more comfort that initial NNARR guidance (YoY growth) leaves room for upside—risk-reward remains balanced," said analysts led by Richard Poland.

The analysts noted that, given mixed performance versus guidance in recent years, the initial fiscal year 2027 outlook could leave limited room for upside.

Related Stocks: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and Check Point Software Technologies (CHKP) each rose about 1%, while Zscaler (ZS) and CrowdStrike (CRWD) were largely flat but in the green. Fortinet (FTNT) was largely flat but in the red premarket on Friday.
